title: Bounded Prefetch, Worker Ownership, Ordering, and Backpressure · Stakeholder Go Mission
summary: A wildlife-education ranger cohort using unreliable field connections. They need Pokemon detail, catch, owned inspection, location encounters, recoverable errors, bounded prefetch, Ctrl-C cleanup, and unchanged-state guarantees when work fails. Planned artifact: Deliver Cancellable Ranger Encounter Notebook with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and…
objectives: Model stable identity, species, types, abilities, stats, dimensions, and optional fields separately from the transport DTO.
Store a deliberately owned domain value rather than a transport pointer or cache alias and define mutation policy.
Deduplicate Pokemon by stable identity while retaining area, version, method, chance, or condition provenance needed by the view.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Commit to the mission model · Project Pokedex Go Detail Domain M…
steps.instruction:
project case — A project Go team handles case 1872 during a fair-use API stewardship review. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce prefetch concurrency, queue, ordering, cancellation, and partial-failure ownership; failed and canceled commands must preserve the prior admitted state; then change one command, argument, EOF, or output-width condition and preserve the session contract. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Pokedex Go Channel Owne…
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: pokedex-go-channel-ownership-uvjesq" that uses case 9455 to enforce prefetch concurrency, queue, ordering, cancellation, and partial-failure ownership. Keep defaults runnable and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact PokéAPI, read or write host files, handle real terminal or OS signals, spawn unbounded work, or claim race, leak, load, accessibility, cross-platform, package, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, original fixtures, controlled RoundTrippers and httptest servers, race and fuzz runs, temporary files, subprocess and signal harnesses, inspected artifacts, representative platforms, accessibility review, capacity, rollback, recovery, and release gates.
